mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of co-positive growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and supply-side strength, leading to positive short-term equity market momentum.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during high inflation periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly correlates with rising corporate revenues and profit margins, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL breaching the threshold, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of threshold-crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production signal synchronized economic strength, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal underperforms individual series, or 20-day forward returns are negative in over 50% of synchronized growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a credit tightening cycle indicate weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline but equity markets remain flat or positive, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) outside of credit tightening periods.
